perturbation.original_text: If any of this Agreement is more restrictive than permitted by law in any jurisdiction in which enforcement is sought, this Agreement will be limited only to the extent necessary to bring this Agreement within the law of such jurisdiction and other provisions of the Agreement will remain in force.
perturbation.changed_text: If any of this Agreement is more restrictive than permitted by law in any jurisdiction in which enforcement is sought, this Agreement will be limited only to the extent necessary to bring this Agreement within the law of such jurisdiction and other provisions of the Agreement will remain in force, *unless the parties mutually agree otherwise*.
perturbation.explanation: The addition of '*unless the parties mutually agree otherwise*' introduces a significant exception to the severability clause. The original clause ensures that if a provision is found to be overly restrictive, it will be limited to comply with the law while the rest of the agreement remains in effect. However, the added exception allows the parties to override this automatic adjustment through mutual agreement, potentially enforcing an illegal or unenforceable provision. This creates a contradiction by allowing the parties to choose to uphold something that the original clause aims to correct, thus introducing uncertainty about the enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: GENERAL: The obligations under the Agreement are binding upon the heirs, assigns and legal representatives. This Agreement is governed by the laws of Pennsylvania. If any of this Agreement is more restrictive than permitted by law in any jurisdiction in which enforcement is sought, this Agreement will be limited only to the extent necessary to bring this Agreement within the law of such jurisdiction and other provisions of the Agreement will remain in force, *unless the parties mutually agree otherwise*.
perturbation.justification: The original text included a severability clause, ensuring that if any part of the agreement was overly restrictive, it would be limited to comply with the law, and the rest of the agreement would remain in force. The changed text adds '*unless the parties mutually agree otherwise*' to this clause. This addition allows the parties to override the severability clause and potentially enforce an illegal or unenforceable provision. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but it highlights the contradictory nature of the added phrase. The changed text introduces a direct conflict because it permits the enforcement of something that the original clause seeks to correct, creating uncertainty and a logical inconsistency about enforceability. This aligns with the Structural Flaws definition, as the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
